items.title: Japan core inflation rate in June creeps up from 4-year low as higher oil prices bite
items.summary: This is the first rise in core inflation since March, and was in line with the1.6% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters.

zh-CN.title: 由于油价上涨，日本 6 月份核心通胀率从 4 年低点攀升
zh-CN.summary: 这是核心通胀自3月份以来首次上升，与路透社调查的经济学家预期的1.6%的增长相符。

ja.title: 原油価格高騰を受け、6月の日本のコアインフレ率は4年ぶりの低水準から徐々に上昇
ja.summary: これは３月以来初めてのコアインフレ率の上昇で、ロイターがまとめたエコノミスト予想の１．６％成長と一致した。

zh-TW.title: 由於油價上漲，日本 6 月核心通膨率從 4 年低點攀升
zh-TW.summary: 這是核心通膨自3月以來首次上升，與路透社調查的經濟學家預期的1.6%的成長相符。

ko.title: 일본 6월 근원 인플레이션율은 유가 상승으로 인해 4년 만에 최저치에서 소폭 상승
ko.summary: 이는 3월 이후 처음으로 근원 인플레이션이 상승한 것이며, 로이터가 조사한 경제학자들의 예상 성장률인 1.6%와 일치하는 수치입니다.

de.title: Die japanische Kerninflationsrate steigt im Juni aufgrund höherer Ölpreise vom 4-Jahres-Tief an
de.summary: Dies ist der erste Anstieg der Kerninflation seit März und entsprach dem von Reuters befragten Ökonomen erwarteten Wachstum von 1,6 %.

fr.title: Le taux d'inflation sous-jacente du Japon en juin augmente par rapport à son plus bas niveau depuis 4 ans alors que la hausse des prix du pétrole se fait sentir
fr.summary: Il s'agit de la première hausse de l'inflation sous-jacente depuis mars, et elle est conforme à la croissance de 1,6% attendue par les économistes interrogés par Reuters.

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

zh-CN.title: 油价飙升重燃通胀担忧 10年期国债收益率升至2025年1月以来最高
zh-CN.summary: 随着油价走高，美国国债收益率周三攀升，交易员也对每周失业保险申请人数下降做出反应。

ko.title: 유가 급등으로 인플레이션 우려가 다시 불붙으면서 10년 만기 국채 수익률은 2025년 1월 이후 최고치로 상승
ko.summary: 미 국채 수익률은 유가 상승에 따라 수요일 실업보험 주간 청구 부진에 트레이더들이 반응하면서 상승했습니다.

ja.title: 原油高騰でインフレ懸念が再燃、10年米国債利回りが2025年1月以来の高水準に上昇
ja.summary: 原油価格の上昇を受けて水曜日の米国債利回りは上昇し、トレーダーらは毎週の失業保険申請件数の低迷にも反応した。

zh-TW.title: 油價飆升重燃通膨擔憂 10年期公債殖利率升至2025年1月以來最高
zh-TW.summary: 隨著油價走高，美國公債殖利率週三攀升，交易員也對每週失業保險申請人數下降做出反應。

fr.title: Le rendement du Trésor à 10 ans atteint son plus haut niveau depuis janvier 2025 alors que la hausse du pétrole ravive la peur de l'inflation
fr.summary: Les rendements du Trésor américain ont grimpé mercredi, suite à la hausse des prix du pétrole, les traders ayant également réagi à une baisse des demandes hebdomadaires d'assurance-chômage.

de.title: Die Rendite 10-jähriger Staatsanleihen steigt auf den höchsten Stand seit Januar 2025, da der steigende Ölpreis die Angst vor Inflation neu entfacht
de.summary: Die Renditen von US-Staatsanleihen stiegen am Mittwoch, nachdem die Ölpreise gestiegen waren, da Händler auch auf einen Rückgang der wöchentlichen Anträge auf Arbeitslosenversicherung reagierten.

items.title: Odds of Federal Reserve rate hike surge as oil prices rip higher
items.summary: Investors are increasingly readying for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates in September.

ja.title: 原油価格の高騰でFRBの利上げ確率が上昇
ja.summary: 投資家は米連邦準備理事会（ＦＲＢ）が９月に利上げに踏み切ることへの備えを強めている。

zh-CN.title: 随着油价上涨，美联储加息的可能性激增
zh-CN.summary: 投资者越来越多地为美联储在九月加息做好准备。

ko.title: 유가 급등으로 연준 금리 인상 가능성 커져
ko.summary: 투자자들은 연준이 9월에 금리를 인상할 것을 점점 더 준비하고 있습니다.

zh-TW.title: 隨著油價上漲，聯準會升息的可能性激增
zh-TW.summary: 投資者越來越多地為聯準會在九月升息做好準備。

fr.title: Les chances d’une hausse des taux de la Réserve fédérale augmentent à mesure que les prix du pétrole augmentent
fr.summary: Les investisseurs se préparent de plus en plus à une hausse des taux d’intérêt par la Réserve fédérale en septembre.

de.title: Die Wahrscheinlichkeit einer Zinserhöhung durch die Federal Reserve steigt, da die Ölpreise steigen
de.summary: Die Anleger bereiten sich zunehmend darauf vor, dass die Federal Reserve im September die Zinsen anheben wird.

items.summary: Personal income increased $181.6 billion (0.7 percent at a monthly rate) in May, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-increased $164.9 billion (0.7 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $156.1 billion (0.7 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $159.9 billion in May.

items.summary: Personal income increased $86.2 billion (0.3 percent at a monthly rate) in December, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-increased $75.7 billion (0.3 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $91.0 billion (0.4 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $90.2 billion in December.

items.title: GDP (Advance Estimate), 4th Quarter and Year 2025
items.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased at an annual rate of 1.4 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025 (October, November, and December), according to the advance estimate released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. In the third quarter, real GDP increased 4.4 percent. The contributors to the increase in real GDP in the fourth quarter were increases in consumer spending and investment. items.summary: Real personal consumption expenditures (PCE) by state increased in 48 states and the District of Columbia in 2024, with the percent change ranging from 5.3 percent in Massachusetts to -0.2 percent in Montana. items.title: Gross Domestic Product by State and Personal Income by State, 3rd Quarter 2025
items.summary: Real gross domestic product (GDP) increased in all 50 states and the District of Columbia in the third quarter of 2025. State-level changes ranged from a 6.5 percent increase in Kansas to a 0.4 percent increase in North Dakota. Personal income increased in all 50 states and the District of Columbia in the third quarter of 2025. items.title: U.S. International Transactions, 3rd Quarter 2025
items.summary: The U.S. current-account deficit narrowed by $22.8 billion, or 9.2 percent, to $226.4 billion in the third quarter of 2025, according to statistics released today by BEA. The revised second-quarter deficit was $249.2 billion. The third-quarter deficit was 2.9 percent of current-dollar gross domestic product, down from 3.3 percent in the second quarter. Full Text

items.summary: Today, the U.S Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) released official statistics of real state personal consumption expenditures (PCE) for the first time. Real PCE decreased 3.8 percent in 2020 for the nation. Across states, the percent change ranged from 2.2 percent in Utah to -7.0 percent in Hawaii, Maryland, and New York; the percent change was -8.9 percent in the District of Columbia.
